seo.title: Tesamorelin Treatment Guide: Egrifta, HIV Lipodystrophy, and Provider Paths
seo.description: US-first tesamorelin treatment hub covering Egrifta for HIV-associated lipodystrophy, visceral fat reduction, cost friction, provider selection, and clinical evidence.

trust.disclaimer: This hub summarizes official tesamorelin treatment pathways at a high level. Indication fit, coverage, ongoing access, and dosing decisions still require confirmation from current official sources and a licensed clinician.

marketSummary: In the United States, tesamorelin is approved as Egrifta for reduction of excess abdominal fat in HIV-infected patients with lipodystrophy. It is also studied for liver fat reduction and cognitive preservation in aging.
overview: Tesamorelin is a synthetic GHRH analog that stimulates the pituitary to release endogenous growth hormone. Unlike direct GH injections, tesamorelin preserves the natural pulsatile GH pattern and feedback axis. Its FDA-approved indication is HIV-associated lipodystrophy, but clinical interest extends to NAFLD and cognitive aging.

approvedProducts.name: Egrifta
approvedProducts.summary: Branded tesamorelin for HIV-associated lipodystrophy. Dosed at 2 mg SC once daily. Requires reconstitution before injection.

approvedProducts.name: Egrifta SV
approvedProducts.summary: Same tesamorelin peptide as Egrifta in a different formulation. Used for the same indication with the same dosing schedule.

benefits: FDA-approved reduction of visceral adipose tissue in HIV-associated lipodystrophy
Demonstrated reduction in liver fat fraction (NAFLD) in HIV-infected patients
Preserves natural growth hormone pulsatility via pituitary stimulation
Emerging evidence for cognitive function preservation in aging adults
sideEffects: Injection site reactions including erythema, pruritus, and pain are common
Fluid retention, peripheral edema, and arthralgia may occur
Treatment should be discontinued if no reduction in visceral fat after 6 months
Not indicated for general weight loss or cosmetic fat reduction outside approved populations
costSummary: Tesamorelin costs vary by fulfillment channel and insurance. As a specialty medication for HIV lipodystrophy, it may be covered under some plans but often requires prior authorization and documentation of HIV status and lipodystrophy diagnosis.
providerSummary: The highest-value next step is finding a provider experienced in HIV metabolic care or hormone optimization who can evaluate whether tesamorelin fits the patient's clinical profile and insurance constraints.
appSummary: Daily tesamorelin dosing creates tracking demand for injection-site rotation, symptom logging, and 6-month efficacy reassessment reminders.

faqs.question: Is tesamorelin only for HIV patients?
faqs.answer: FDA approval is specifically for HIV-infected patients with lipodystrophy. Off-label use for other populations (such as general aging or NAFLD) is not FDA-approved and should be discussed with a qualified clinician.

faqs.question: How does tesamorelin compare to sermorelin?
faqs.answer: Both are GHRH analogs, but tesamorelin has a trans-3-hexenoic acid modification that increases receptor binding stability. Tesamorelin is FDA-approved for HIV lipodystrophy; sermorelin's approved brand (Geref) was discontinued, though sermorelin remains available through compounding.

faqs.question: What is the safest next step before starting tesamorelin?
faqs.answer: Confirm the official indication fit, verify realistic cost and coverage friction, and then use a licensed provider route experienced in HIV metabolic care or hormone optimization.

cta.notes: Use the provider matcher when tesamorelin already looks like the right treatment family; use the cost guide when you still need to compare the path.
